limits: Graph-neighborhood candidate inclusion remains disabled by default.
The standalone context-pack CLI requires an explicit target artifact ID when enabled.
The first exposure uses existing discovered artifacts and authoritative artifact-lineage relations only.
No external edge-record or future-edge-seed input is exposed.
No automatic target inference is performed.
Does not modify ask_provider.py, compare_providers.py, prompt_context.py, provider prompts, or prompt rendering.
Does not enable automatic graph-local context inclusion outside explicit CLI opt-in.
Does not persist graph state or introduce retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production index mutation, memory-store mutation, or runtime-manifest mutation.
Does not by itself satisfy or close the broader GAP-0002 graph-local scoped-memory gap.
name: Graph-neighborhood context-pack CLI exposure
recommended_next_actions: Verify PLAN-20260716-0001 completion through a separate verification and warrant checkpoint.
Keep GAP-0002 open and evaluate actual graph-local memory quality separately from CLI reachability.
Treat any provider-facing exposure, automatic target inference, external edge input, or automatic inclusion as a separate governed slice.
risks: Users may mistake explicit CLI candidate inclusion for complete graph-local scoped-memory selection.
A target ID with sparse or unsuitable artifact lineage may yield no useful neighborhood candidates.
Future provider exposure or automatic target selection could broaden runtime behavior and requires separate governance.
summary: Exposes CAP-0009 through the standalone build_context_pack.py CLI using explicit, default-off graph-neighborhood options. The CLI requires a target artifact ID when enabled, optionally accepts maximum depth and a graph-neighborhood token budget, and derives candidates from existing authoritative artifact lineage without changing provider paths or default context behavior.
